title: WASC operation framework — the canonical run process
question: What is the exact step sequence and structure of the WASC operation framework's per-task run process?
method: claude-history extraction of the last complete end-to-end run (session 6e98b2bc) and a loop run; verbatim tool-call and agent-dispatch sequencing; cross-checked against the operation-system bundle.
findings_summary: The WASC operation framework runs a per-task canonical pipeline: EnterPlanMode, dispatch a read-only Explore agent, the orchestrator independently corroborates the Explore findings against source, write the plan, ExitPlanMode approval, file the task plus edges in .context, branch off main, dispatch a fresh gate-first IMPL agent, the orchestrator re-runs the deterministic gate itself (it does not inherit the IMPL's green), then a separate adversarial AUDIT agent verifies each acceptance criterion and ends with the literal token 'Total findings: N'. Done is two external binary signals: deterministic gate exit 0 AND audit findings 0 (DEC-58); the model may not self-declare done. On findings > 0 each finding re-enters the same pipeline (corroborate, fresh re-IMPL scoped to the finding's whole class, fresh re-audit both-directions) until a pass finds nothing. On done, the machine cascade files a verification item, adds the verification_verifies_item edge, completes the task, closes the gap, runs context-validate, then fast-forward merges to main and updates the spine. Three roles stay separate (Explore read-only, IMPL writes, AUDIT adversarial). The loop's terminator is external, not the model's judgment; that is why it completes. Derived from the last complete run (175 steps, session 6e98b2bc), which audited clean on the first pass.
